name: Kusaal
notes: Kusaal (Kʋsaal), a Gur / Mabia (Atlantic-Congo) language of north-eastern Ghana. INPUT CONTRACT: text is assumed to be in the UNIFIED KUSAAL ORTHOGRAPHY in use since 2013 (the orthography of the 2015+ Bible revision, 'B3' in Eddyshaw's grammar), for the AGOLLE dialect — the literary standard. Toende Kusaal differs phonologically and is not covered. KEY FEATURES: (1) SEVEN SHORT MONOPHTHONGS /a ɛ i ɪ ɔ u ʊ/. The reformed orthography writes ⟨ʋ⟩ for /ʊ/ and ⟨ɛ ɔ⟩ for the open mid vowels, but it still uses ⟨i⟩ for BOTH /i/ and /ɪ/, so that contrast is not recoverable from spelling (⟨i⟩ maps to [i] first, [ɪ] as an alternative; older ⟨ɩ⟩ where it occurs is [ɪ]). ⟨e⟩ and ⟨o⟩ inside diphthongs are graphic variants of /ɪ/ and /ʊ/. Long vowels are written doubled; length is not always spelled. (2) LABIAL-VELARS ⟨kp gb⟩ = /k͡p ɡ͡b/. (3) NASALISED SEMIVOWELS: ⟨ny⟩ = nasalised [j̃] and ⟨nw⟩ = nasalised [w̃] (Toende speakers may retain [ɲ] and [ŋ͡m]). (4) The apostrophe ⟨'⟩ marks vowel GLOTTALISATION, not a consonant — it is realised as creaky voice or a glottal approximant after the first mora; it is mapped to [ʔ] as the closest segmental approximation. (5) ⟨h⟩ is phonemic only in loanwords, and is also a frequent allophone of non-initial /s/; ⟨r⟩ is a flap/approximant [ɾ] and never word-initial; /k t p/ are aspirated word-initially, and word-internal ⟨k t p ŋ⟩ are geminates. (6) TONE: Kusaal is a TONE LANGUAGE (terraced-level system with downstep). The standard orthography DOES NOT MARK TONE (nor nasalisation, nor vowel length consistently), so tone is NOT RECOVERABLE from orthographic input and is not modelled here.

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
